title: Peer Counseling - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Mental & Behavioral Health > Peer Counseling 22 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - No Filter Breast Cancer Breastfeeding Individuals Perinatal Depression Reproductive Issues Sexual Assault Issues Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness 211INFO 211INFO MATERNAL AND CHILD HEALTH PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF 211info's Maternal and Child Health Program provides specialized information, referral, and follow-up services to individuals and family members of all genders covering needs and questions related to pregnancy, maternal and child health, sexual and reproductive health, and women's health across the life span, including resources for women with breast cancer. Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-6pm Email 211 Visit Website More Details ALL-OPTIONS ALL-OPTIONS TALKLINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a confidential space to talk about pregnancy, parenting, abortion, and adoption. Peer counselors offer judgment-free support at any point before, during, and after pregnancy, regardless of which options the caller is considering. Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 7am-10pm, Saturday-Sunday 7am-3pm Email (888) 493-0092 Visit Website More Details BENTON COUNTY BEHAVIORAL HEALTH HEALTH SERVICE BUILDING SUBSTANCE USE DISORDER TREATMENT PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers individual and group counseling for people who want to change their relationship to substance use. Services may also include drug and alcohol assessments, drug screening, and case management. Peer support and recovery mentors are available, along with referrals to mental health and community resources. Also supports parent–child reunification, provides Medication-Assisted Treatment (MAT), helps with admissions to detox and residential treatment, and creates relapse prevention plans to support long-term recovery. 530 NW 27th Street Corvallis, OR 97330 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday -Friday 8am-5pm (541) 766-6767 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BREAST FRIENDS BREAST CANCER PEER SUPPORT NETWORK AND RESOURCES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides one-on-one support through diagnosis, treatment, and beyond. Manages a volunteer match program for individuals needing additional support. Hats, wigs, prostheses, and bras are available for chemotherapy-related hair loss and post-mastectomy needs. In-person and phone support available. Offers workshops, retreats, and support groups. 11481 SW Hall Boulevard Suite 105 Tigard, OR 97223 Eligibility: Newly-diagnosed breast cancer patients Hours: Monday-Thursday 10am-4pm, Friday by appointment Email (503) 598-8048 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CASCADE AIDS PROJECT PEER-TO-PEER SUPPORT SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides supportive services for people living with HIV/AIDS, mental health challenges, or substance use issues. Helps individuals connect with healthcare, mental health, and drug and alcohol recovery services 520 NW Davis Street Suite 215 Portland, OR 97209 Eligibility: Individuals with an HIV/AIDS diagnosis, income at or below 300% of the federal poverty level, needing extra mental health or substance use support, currently housed but struggling to maintain housing, or needing help connecting with services. Hours: Monday-Thursday 9am-5pm Email (503) 223-5907 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CLACKAMAS COUNTY BEHAVIORAL HEALTH DIVISION 24/7 MOBILE CRISIS RESPONSE TEAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides in-person therapeutic responses as well as peer support services in public settings where a mental health crisis is occurring, including an individual's home, school, business and on the street. Mobile crisis teams consist of clinicians, care coordinators and peer support specialists. 2051 Kaen Road Suite 154 Oregon City, OR 97045 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week (503) 655-8585 247 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FORA HEALTH ADULT TREATMENT CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers Dual Diagnosis Treatment (DDT) facility including withdrawal management (detox), Medications for Addiction Treatment (MAT), and residential treatment including medically-monitored residential treatment, outpatient, intensive outpatient, and DUII. For patients in treatment, we offer peer mentors and family therapy. 10230 SE Cherry Blossom Drive Portland, OR 97216 Eligibility: Age 18 and older with primary diagnosis of alcohol or drug addictive disorders Hours: Office: Monday-Friday 7:30am-5:30am, Saturday 7:30am-noon Withdrawal Management: Monday-Sunday 7:30am walk up MAT: Monday-Friday 9am-4pm Outpatient enrollment: Tuesday 2pm-4pm, Thursday 10:30am-2pm, Friday 11am-noon Weekends: OHP patients only Email (503) 535-1151 Get Directions Visit Website More Details GOOD SAMARITAN MINISTRIES INTERNATIONAL HEADQUARTERS COUNSELING MINISTRY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF A faith-based organization that offers a counseling ministry that includes individual, marriage, and men's and women's groups. The groups are run by trained peer counselors. 7929 SW Cirrus Drive Beaverton, OR 97008 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Thursday 11am-8pm, Friday 11am-5pm Email (503) 644-2339 Get Directions Visit Website More Details KAIROS KAIROS CADENZA RESIDENTIAL TREATMENT HOME RESIDENTIAL TREATMENT HOMES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a residential treatment home that houses five young adults with serious mental and behavioral disorders. The facility is non-secure. Residents receive intensive mental health services geared toward helping them develop the thinking and coping skills that will assist them in making the transition to adulthood. Staff help them learn to navigate the work and/or education worlds and to develop skills that enable them to attend school, work, and live independently in the community. 4385 Sunnyside Road NE Salem, OR 97305 Eligibility: Ages 17-24 Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 520-1332 Get Directions Visit Website More Details KAIROS KAIROS MOMENTUM RESIDENTIAL TREATMENT HOME RESIDENTIAL TREATMENT HOMES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a residential treatment home that houses five young adults with serious mental and behavioral disorders. The facility is non-secure. Residents receive intensive mental health services geared toward helping them develop the thinking and coping skills that will assist them in making the transition to adulthood. Staff help them learn to navigate the work and/or education worlds and to develop skills that enable them to attend school, work, and live independently in the community. 1920 Kurtz Lane Grants Pass, OR 97526 Eligibility: Ages 17-24 Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 520-1332 Get Directions Visit Website More Details KAIROS KAIROS TEMPO RESIDENTIAL TREATMENT HOME RESIDENTIAL TREATMENT HOMES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a residential treatment home that houses five young adults with serious mental and behavioral disorders. The facility is non-secure. Residents receive intensive mental health services geared toward helping them develop the thinking and coping skills that will assist them in making the transition to adulthood. Staff help them learn to navigate the work and/or education worlds and to develop skills that enable them to attend school, work, and live independently in the community. 348 Ruby Avenue Eugene, OR 97404 Eligibility: Ages 17-24 Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 520-1332 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LIFEWAYS INC PEER SUPPORT CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Partners with Lifeways to provide a range of services and support to mental health consumers, including self-help groups, skill groups, advocacy, activities, peer-to-peer support, food bank, linkage to community resources, dual diagnosis group, and National Alliance for Mental Illness (NAMI) services. 702 Sunset Drive Ontario, OR 97914 Eligibility: Individuals experiencing mental health issues Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-3pm Email (541) 889-9167 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NEW NARRATIVE NEW NARRATIVE MILL PARK CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides mental health services, including case management, cognitive and creative therapy, skills training, employment assistance, assistance obtaining benefits and entitlements, prescriber services, medication monitoring (must be an active participant in behavioral health counseling), money management, medical coordination, and peer-delivered services. Cannot provide neurological assessments for ADHD or autism. Currently unable to offer medication management to new intakes. 1952 SE 122nd Avenue Portland, OR 97233 Eligibility: Individuals with severe and persistent mental health conditions Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4:30pm Email (503) 726-3764 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NEW NARRATIVE NEW NARRATIVE SPRING CREEK CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides mental health services, including case management, cognitive and creative therapy, skills training, employment assistance, assistance obtaining benefits and entitlements, prescriber services, medication monitoring (must be an active participant in behavioral health counseling), money management, medical coordination, and peer-delivered services. Cannot provide neurological assessments for ADHD or autism. Currently unable to offer medication management to new intakes. 8915 SW Center Street Tigard, OR 97223 Eligibility: Individuals with severe and persistent mental health conditions Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4:30pm Email (503) 726-3732 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NORTHWEST HUMAN SERVICES HOMELESS OUTREACH & ADVOCACY PROJECT HOMELESS OUTREACH AND ADVOCACY PROJECT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a drop-in center. Services include information and referral, laundry, clothing, showers, lunch, mailbox access, mental health support groups, and peer support services. Also connects clients with medical services at West Salem Medical Clinic. 694 Church Street NE Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Individuals who are homeless or at risk of becoming homeless Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-1pm and 2pm-4pm Appointments: Monday-Friday 2pm-4pm Email (503) 588-5827 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ONE COMMUNITY HEALTH HOOD RIVER CLINIC SALUD DIABETES MANAGEMENT PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides assistance in navigating lifestyle changes to reduce diabetes risks. Program participants receive coaching, personal nutrition care plans, and medication management in one-on-one or group settings. 849 Pacific Avenue Hood River, OR 97031 Eligibility: Individuals who are established Community One Health patients Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 386-6380 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ONE COMMUNITY HEALTH THE DALLES CLINIC SALUD DIABETES MANAGEMENT PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides assistance in navigating lifestyle changes to reduce diabetes risks. Program participants receive coaching, personal nutrition care plans, and medication management in one-on-one or group settings. 1040 Webber Street The Dalles, OR 97058 Eligibility: Individuals who are established Community One Health patients Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 296-4610 Get Directions Visit Website More Details RIMROCK TRAILS TREATMENT SERVICES BEND COUNSELING CLINIC COUNSELING SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides outpatient mental health and substance use disorder counseling, including screenings and assessments, treatment for eating disorders, and individual, couples, group, family counseling, peer support, and case care management. In-person and Telehealth treatment options are available. Medication management is available through tele-psychiatry. 548 SW 13th Street Bend, OR 97702 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Thursday 8:30am-5:30pm Email (541) 388-8459 Get Directions Visit Website More Details RIMROCK TRAILS TREATMENT SERVICES PRINEVILLE COUNSELING CLINIC COUNSELING SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides outpatient mental health and substance use disorder counseling, including screenings and assessments, treatment for eating disorders, and individual, couples, group, family counseling, peer support, and case care management. In-person and Telehealth treatment options are available. Medication management is available through tele-psychiatry. 200 SE Combs Flat Road Prineville, OR 97754 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-5pm Email (541) 388-8459 Get Directions Visit Website More Details RIMROCK TRAILS TREATMENT SERVICES REDMOND COUNSELING CLINIC COUNSELING SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides outpatient mental health and substance use disorder counseling, including screenings and assessments, treatment for eating disorders, and individual, couples, group, family counseling, peer support, and case care management. In-person and Telehealth treatment options are available. Medication management is available through tele-psychiatry. 215 SW 7th Street Redmond, OR 97756 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-5pm Email (541) 388-8459 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SEXUAL ASSAULT SUPPORT SERVICES OF LANE COUNTY SEXUAL ASSAULT SUPPORT SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides support for survivors of past and present sexual assault, abuse, and harassment and for friends and family of survivors. Services include a crisis line, medical response and legal advocacy, support groups, peer support, community education and outreach for prevention, and information and referral. All services are confidential. 1902 Jefferson Street Suite 1 Eugene, OR 97405 Eligibility: Survivors of sexual assault, abuse, and harassment, and their friends and family members Hours: -Crisis line, advocacy, legal, and medical response: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week -All other services: Hours vary (844) 404-7700 Get Directions Visit Website More Details WELLMAMA MATERNAL MENTAL HEALTH AND SUPPORT SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides peer support, support groups, and wellness services to people affected by or at risk for mental health conditions related to parenthood. Support people, including partners and babies under age 1, are welcome at all meetings. 576 Olive Street Suite 205 Eugene, OR 97401 Eligibility: Families struggling with emotions related to reproductive health, such as pregnancy, the first few years postpartum, and perinatal loss Hours: Support group times vary Email (541) 525-0495 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BENTON COUNTY BEHAVIORAL HEALTH SUBSTANCE USE DISORDER TREATMENT PROGRAM BREAST FRIENDS BREAST CANCER PEER SUPPORT NETWORK AND RESOURCES CASCADE AIDS PROJECT PEER-TO-PEER SUPPORT SERVICES CLACKAMAS COUNTY BEHAVIORAL HEALTH DIVISION 24/7 MOBILE CRISIS RESPONSE TEAM FORA HEALTH ADULT TREATMENT CENTER GOOD SAMARITAN MINISTRIES INTERNATIONAL HEADQUARTERS COUNSELING MINISTRY KAIROS RESIDENTIAL TREATMENT HOMES KAIROS RESIDENTIAL TREATMENT HOMES KAIROS RESIDENTIAL TREATMENT HOMES LIFEWAYS INC PEER SUPPORT CENTER NEW NARRATIVE NEW NARRATIVE MILL PARK CENTER NEW NARRATIVE NEW NARRATIVE SPRING CREEK CENTER NORTHWEST HUMAN SERVICES HOMELESS OUTREACH AND ADVOCACY PROJECT ONE COMMUNITY HEALTH SALUD DIABETES MANAGEMENT PROGRAM ONE COMMUNITY HEALTH SALUD DIABETES MANAGEMENT PROGRAM RIMROCK TRAILS TREATMENT SERVICES COUNSELING SERVICES RIMROCK TRAILS TREATMENT SERVICES COUNSELING SERVICES RIMROCK TRAILS TREATMENT SERVICES COUNSELING SERVICES SEXUAL ASSAULT SUPPORT SERVICES OF LANE COUNTY SEXUAL ASSAULT SUPPORT SERVICES WELLMAMA MATERNAL MENTAL HEALTH AND SUPPORT SERVICES
